question: A 55-year-old woman presents with crushing substernal chest pain radiating to left arm for 2 hours. Diaphoretic. ECG shows ST elevation of 3mm in leads V1-V4 with reciprocal ST depression in II, III, aVF.

explanation.why_correct: ST elevation in V1-V4 = anterior STEMI (LAD occlusion). Reciprocal changes confirm transmural ischemia.

why_wrong.NSTEMI: NSTEMI shows ST depression/T inversion, NOT ST elevation.
why_wrong.Unstable Angina: UA has normal troponin + no ST elevation.
why_wrong.Pericarditis: Pericarditis has DIFFUSE ST elevation + PR depression, not localized to V1-V4.
why_wrong.Aortic Dissection: Dissection causes tearing pain + pulse deficit + widened mediastinum, not ST elevation.

question: A 72-year-old man presents with crushing substernal chest pain radiating to left arm for 2 hours. Diaphoretic. ECG shows ST elevation of 3mm in leads V1-V4 with reciprocal ST depression in II, III, aVF.

explanation.why_correct: ST elevation in V1-V4 = anterior STEMI (LAD occlusion). Reciprocal changes confirm transmural ischemia.

why_wrong.NSTEMI: NSTEMI shows ST depression/T inversion, NOT ST elevation.
why_wrong.Unstable Angina: UA has normal troponin + no ST elevation.
why_wrong.Pericarditis: Pericarditis has DIFFUSE ST elevation + PR depression, not localized to V1-V4.
why_wrong.Aortic Dissection: Dissection causes tearing pain + pulse deficit + widened mediastinum, not ST elevation.

question: A 65-year-old man with palpitations. ECG shows irregularly irregular rhythm with absent P waves. HR 130 bpm.

explanation.why_correct: Irregularly irregular + absent P waves = AFib. RVR = rate >100.

why_wrong.SVT: SVT is regular, narrow QRS, rate 150-250.
why_wrong.Sinus Tachycardia: Sinus tach has P waves before each QRS.

question: A 35-year-old man with palpitations. ECG shows irregularly irregular rhythm with absent P waves. HR 130 bpm.

explanation.why_correct: Irregularly irregular + absent P waves = AFib. RVR = rate >100.

why_wrong.SVT: SVT is regular, narrow QRS, rate 150-250.
why_wrong.Sinus Tachycardia: Sinus tach has P waves before each QRS.

question: A 35-year-old man with progressive dyspnea, orthopnea, and bilateral leg edema. Echo shows LVEF 30% with global hypokinesis.

explanation.why_correct: LVEF <40% + symptoms = HFrEF. Global hypokinesis indicates ischemic or non-ischemic cardiomyopathy.

why_wrong.Diastolic HF: HFpEF has normal EF (>50%) with diastolic dysfunction.
why_wrong.COPD: COPD causes wheezing, hyperinflation, not bilateral edema.
why_wrong.PE: PE causes acute dyspnea, clear lungs, RV strain on echo.
why_wrong.Nephrotic: Nephrotic causes edema + proteinuria, not low EF.

question: A 55-year-old woman presents with crushing substernal chest pain radiating to left arm for 2 hours. Diaphoretic. ECG shows ST elevation of 3mm in leads V1-V4 with reciprocal ST depression in II, III, aVF.

explanation.why_correct: ST elevation in V1-V4 = anterior STEMI (LAD occlusion). Reciprocal changes confirm transmural ischemia.

why_wrong.NSTEMI: NSTEMI shows ST depression/T inversion, NOT ST elevation.
why_wrong.Unstable Angina: UA has normal troponin + no ST elevation.
why_wrong.Pericarditis: Pericarditis has DIFFUSE ST elevation + PR depression, not localized to V1-V4.
why_wrong.Aortic Dissection: Dissection causes tearing pain + pulse deficit + widened mediastinum, not ST elevation.

question: A 45-year-old man with syncope during exercise. ECG shows deep T wave inversion in V1-V3. Family history of sudden cardiac death.

explanation.why_correct: Exercise syncope + T inversion V1-V3 + family history = ARVC. Fatty replacement of RV myocardium.

why_wrong.Brugada: Brugada has coved ST elevation V1-V2, syncope at rest.
why_wrong.Long QT: LQTS has prolonged QTc, T wave alternans.
why_wrong.HCM: HCM has LVH on echo, not RV involvement.
why_wrong.Vasovagal: Vasovagal has triggers (pain, fear), normal ECG.

question: A 55-year-old woman with acute chest pain. BP 85/60, JVD, muffled heart sounds. ECG shows low voltage with electrical alternans.

explanation.why_correct: Beck triad (hypotension + JVD + muffled sounds) + electrical alternans = tamponade. Pericardiocentesis emergently.

why_wrong.Tension PTX: PTX has tracheal deviation, hyperresonance, absent breath sounds.
why_wrong.Massive PE: PE has RV strain on ECG, clear lungs.
why_wrong.Cardiogenic Shock: Cardiogenic has pulmonary edema, not electrical alternans.
why_wrong.Dissection: Dissection has tearing pain, pulse deficit, widened mediastinum.

why_wrong.Pericarditis: Pericarditis has DIFFUSE ST elevation + PR depression, not localized to V1-V4.
why_wrong.Aortic Dissection: Dissection causes tearing pain + pulse deficit + widened mediastinum, not ST elevation.

question: A 35-year-old woman presents with crushing substernal chest pain radiating to left arm for 2 hours. Diaphoretic. ECG shows ST elevation of 3mm in leads V1-V4 with reciprocal ST depression in II, III, aVF.

explanation.why_correct: ST elevation in V1-V4 = anterior STEMI (LAD occlusion). Reciprocal changes confirm transmural ischemia.

why_wrong.NSTEMI: NSTEMI shows ST depression/T inversion, NOT ST elevation.
why_wrong.Unstable Angina: UA has normal troponin + no ST elevation.
why_wrong.Pericarditis: Pericarditis has DIFFUSE ST elevation + PR depression, not localized to V1-V4.
why_wrong.Aortic Dissection: Dissection causes tearing pain + pulse deficit + widened mediastinum, not ST elevation.
